summary: A solid 1995 Charlotte show highlighted by a soaring Foolish Heart and moving Attics of My Life, captured on an audience tape that’s warm but far from pristine.
review: Fans repeatedly praise the performance—Jerry was in top form despite health issues, with standout jams on Foolish Heart, Jack Straw into Bertha, I Want To Tell You, Attics Of My Life and a rousing Mighty Quinn encore. The recording is an audience source; listeners note its warmth and occasional muddiness, as well as a brief post‑Space segment that some find underwhelming. Overall the music shines through the imperfect tape.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers award high marks for the musical performance (average ~4.7 stars) while the audience recording receives mixed comments, pulling the overall weighted score to just over 4 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm but not crystal‑clear; some listeners call it 'good considering' while others note muffled mids, echo‑chamber arena ambience and a short post‑Space segment. No matrix or soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set drags a bit until the Music/Jack Straw momentum kicks in; second set lifts off with Foolish Heart, Attics of My Life and a tight encore. Post‑Space is notably short, which some listeners find disappointing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as 'on' and delivering an amazing Foolish Heart jam despite health issues.
band_member_comments.Drums: Only brief mention that the Drums/Space segment was short.
